triggers.cited_reason: DevHub account access uses wetten.overheid.nl sign-in, and any MFA or security prompt must be completed by the user in the visible browser.
triggers.user_visible_handoff_text: I cannot complete MFA or security prompts. Please handle this step directly in the live browser, then I can continue with read-only review or reversible local draft planning.

triggers.cited_reason: The PP&D automation boundary prohibits bypassing CAPTCHA or bot-defense controls.
triggers.user_visible_handoff_text: I cannot solve or bypass CAPTCHA. Please complete it yourself in the live browser before any further assisted read-only review.

triggers.cited_reason: Account creation and account security changes are outside unattended DevHub automation and require a separate human-run process.
triggers.user_visible_handoff_text: I cannot create or modify a wetten.overheid.nl or DevHub account. Please complete account setup directly, then return for safe review or local preview work.

triggers.cited_reason: Password recovery is an account-security workflow and the worker must not request, store, or recover credentials.
triggers.user_visible_handoff_text: I cannot perform password recovery or ask for credentials. Please complete recovery directly through the official sign-in flow.

triggers.cited_reason: Fee payment is a financial workflow, and final payment execution requires separate guardrails and user action.
triggers.user_visible_handoff_text: I can help review fee notices, but I cannot enter payment details or submit payment. Please execute payment yourself in the official surface.

triggers.cited_reason: Controls with unclear official consequences must be treated as consequential until a source-backed action classification proves they are safe and reversible.
triggers.user_visible_handoff_text: This control could affect an official PP&D or DevHub record, and I do not have enough cited evidence to classify it as safe. Please decide this step yourself in the live surface.

triggers.cited_reason: Official guidance says some PP&D permit types must be submitted by email or other non-DevHub paths, so DevHub cannot be assumed to cover every permit.
triggers.user_visible_handoff_text: This permit path is not supported for DevHub automation. I can show the cited guidance and prepare a local checklist, but the official email, staff-intake, payment, certification, or submission step must be handled by you.

next_safe_alternatives.label: Show the cited PP&D or DevHub guidance that explains why the workflow requires manual handoff.
Review visible permit status, fee notice, correction request, or attachment list without changing the official record.
Prepare a local checklist or draft field map for the user to review before they act in the official surface.
Generate a local PDF or form-field preview that does not upload, submit, certify, schedule, cancel, or pay.
